jokes.text: That sign says 'Applesauce.' No, no, no. I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Hank points to applause sign, misreads it
jokes.punchline: Quick correction reveals the misdirection
jokes.explanation: Simple warm-up crowd work, typical Hank trying too hard. Economical but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That sign says 'Applesauce.' No, no, no. I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'

jokes.text: and now, because it's a condition of his probation, Larry Sanders!
jokes.setup: Standard late-night host introduction format
jokes.punchline: Suggests host is on probation, not appearing by choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the standard introduction format. Perfect economy and dark humor that sets the show's tone.

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: and now, because it's a condition of his probation, Larry Sanders!

jokes.text: He said for a million dollars, he'd let Robert Redford sleep with Hillary.
jokes.setup: Clinton's plan to end the deficit
jokes.punchline: Absurd monetization of first lady
jokes.explanation: Standard political joke of the era. Competent but not groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He said for a million dollars, he'd let Robert Redford sleep with Hillary.

jokes.text: If Robert Redford has to pay a million dollars to get laid... Do I need to finish this thought? Imagine what Hank must have to pay.
jokes.setup: Robert Redford paying for sex setup
jokes.punchline: Hank would have to pay even more
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke with character-specific insult. Well-structured with the pause for effect.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If Robert Redford has to pay a million dollars to get laid... Do I need to finish this thought? Imagine what Hank must have to pay.

jokes.text: I am not watching my show right now. I'm watching Bonanza. That is not Hank. That is Hoss.
jokes.setup: Larry defending himself on phone about watching TV
jokes.punchline: Comparing Hank to Hoss Cartwright
jokes.explanation: Clever visual comparison that shows Larry's pettiness and Hank's physical presence. Character-driven humor.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I am not watching my show right now. I'm watching Bonanza. That is not Hank. That is Hoss.

jokes.text: Why do you put her through? Larry, you called her. Oh, that's right.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about his wife calling
jokes.punchline: Beverly reveals he called her
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Larry's self-deception. Beverly's deadpan delivery makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Why do you put her through?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Larry, you called her.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Oh, that's right.

jokes.text: Do I have trouble prioritizing emotional commitments? Oh, please, don't ask me. I haven't had a healthy relationship since junior high.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Beverly's brutal honesty about her own failures
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Beverly, showing her self-awareness and deadpan humor.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Do I have trouble prioritizing emotional commitments?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Oh, please, don't ask me. I haven't had a healthy relationship since junior high.

jokes.text: Well, let's stick to me today.
jokes.setup: Beverly's relationship confession
jokes.punchline: Larry's narcissistic redirect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - his total self-absorption delivered with casual cruelty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, let's stick to me today.

jokes.text: Jesus! Are you laying in your own puke?
jokes.setup: Artie enters Larry's office
jokes.punchline: Larry is in terrible physical state
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing Larry's deteriorated state. Artie's bluntness works.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Jesus! Are you laying in your own puke?

jokes.text: Who are they? It's a mystery. They don't need to be a mystery to me, Artie. I'm the host.

jokes.punchline: Larry's obvious frustration at being kept in dark
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing the dysfunction between Larry and Artie.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Who are they? It's a mystery.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They don't need to be a mystery to me, Artie. I'm the host.

jokes.text: I'll tell you who got nominated. Letterman, Saturday Night Live, and I'll bet you Billy Crystal hosting the Academy Awards. Am I right? What's the other one? A Jackie Mason Hanukkah, Artie?

jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Jackie Mason Hanukkah special

dialogue.speaker: Artie

jokes.text: Walk it off? Cancel. Move on. Why don't I just put ice on it? It's a marriage, for god's sakes!
jokes.setup: Artie's advice to walk off marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous sports injury comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing the contrast between Artie's crude pragmatism and Larry's emotional needs.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Walk it off?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Cancel. Move on. Why don't I just put ice on it? It's a marriage, for god's sakes!

jokes.punchline: Increasingly manic reasoning about towel availability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's neurotic spiral over something mundane. Perfect character writing.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I thought you were talking about the Emmy nominations. Oh, screw her! She was always trying to drive a wedge between Larry and me.

dialogue.speaker: Hank

They're dropping you for Chevy Chase.
You're kidding.
Hard to believe, isn't it?

dialogue.line: Who can afford a hair weave or a transplant? And for that matter, who knows a baboon who wants to give you his hair?

jokes.text: If it's Hank, why isn't Hank here? Hank has one line, and it's his name. You think he can do that without a rehearsal?
jokes.setup: Hank absent from rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Questioning if Hank can say his own name without practice
jokes.explanation: Excellent character assassination showing Larry's contempt for Hank's minimal competence.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If it's Hank, why isn't Hank here? Hank has one line, and it's his name. You think he can do that without a rehearsal?

jokes.text: Isn't 'cap in a can' funnier? Cap in a can. Ha! You're right! That's funnier. Cap in a can!
jokes.setup: Discussing sketch comedy bit title
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate enthusiasm for minor word change
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about comedy writing and Larry's need to feel involved in creative process.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Isn't 'cap in a can' funnier?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Cap in a can. Ha! You're right! That's funnier. Cap in a can!

jokes.text: This doesn't look like it comes from a can. I understand how this part could come from a can, but how would the brim know to come out of the can before the rest of the hat?

jokes.punchline: Larry overthinking the logistics of spray-on hat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's overthinking of obviously absurd comedy prop.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This doesn't look like it comes from a can. I understand how this part could come from a can, but how would the brim know to come out of the can before the rest of the hat?

jokes.text: I'm pissing in my pants, conceptual flaw and all!
jokes.setup: Larry questioning the hat bit logic
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic enthusiasm despite admitting it makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Great line showing Artie's pragmatic approach to comedy - doesn't need to make sense if it's funny.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm pissing in my pants, conceptual flaw and all!

jokes.text: I'm sure that the writers can cook up something funny about your marital disorders.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: He's been working on them since 1989.
